basic_info.headline: Anthropic | Aspiring towards a world with less suffering
basic_info.about:
Building safety research teams @ Anthropic.

- Experience in operations, communications (journalism/digital media/public affairs), and research.
- Interested in social service, policy and media.
- Passionate about animal welfarm, coffee, and effective altruism.

experience.title: Summer Research Fellow
experience.description: Conducting empirical research on LLM properties related to AI conflict/cooperation.

experience.title: Campus Specialist Intern
experience.description:
•	Organized two seven-week Introductory Fellowships (IFs) and our inaugural In-Depth Fellowship for UChicago students interested in critically engaging with effective altruism, a movement and community seeking high-impact opportunities.
•	Grew the UChicago EA group by 1.5x in one year, including increasing core leadership from <.25 FTE to .75 FTE, quadrupling the IF cohort from 8 undergraduates to 32 students across the College and Graduate Schools, and doubling listhost sign-ups.
•	Supported leadership development by identifying, proactively reaching out to, and onboarding 10 promising members from the Autumn IF cohort to facilitate the Winter IF cycle and/or create original projects.
•	Planned for sustainable group succession by training three candidates and creating offboarding documentation.
•	Reworked the leadership structure by delineating tasks into project areas, organizing resources, and delegating responsibilities. This improved UChicago EA’s efficiency and enabled the creation of programming such as weekly socials.
•	Coordinated the creation of UChicago EA’s digital infrastructure such as the website, newsletter, and Facebook page
•	Devised an annual plan and budget to successfully apply for and begin deploying over $7,500 to grow UChicago EA.
•	Co-founded Midwest EA, a collection of university EA groups (Northwestern, UW-Madison, UMichigan), and led our inaugural project of a high-impact careers retreat, intended to support and innovate upon our core fellowship programming.
•	Competencies developed: Program and team leadership, Budget management, Entrepreneurship, Organizational skills

experience.title: New York Public Affairs Intern
experience.description:
•	Juggled daily responsibilities for six clients spanning multiple sectors: the Rhode Island Department of Education, New Yorkers United for Justice, the First Lady of New York City, AT&T New York, and Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.
•	Compiled relevant newspaper and TV clips every morning for internal and client use, enabling my teams to seize timely opportunities such as a rapid response letter from New Yorkers United for Justice to DOCCS.
•	Developed 7 pitches, 10 press releases, and 2 media advisories tailored to different clients and reporters, strengthening my ability to craft narratives using limited data and reframe ideas for diverse audiences.
•	Researched reporters using Cision and Google to create 6 press lists on Google Sheets, ranging from hyperlocal newspapers to national broadcast shows across a range of issues including healthcare, housing, and small business.
•	Competencies developed: Project prioritization, Press outreach, High-speed writing (including drafts and note-taking)

experience.title: Deputy Organizing Lead
experience.company: Pierina Sanchez for New York City Council 2021
experience.description:
Part-time field organizing role:
•	Led the Building Captain initiative by recruiting, training, and managing 20 volunteers to conduct outreach in their apartment buildings, resulting in an additional 100 votes and contributing to the campaign’s victory.
•	Led weekly phone banks and text banks for volunteers
•	Coordinated volunteering logistics by regularly contacting and reaching out to volunteers
•	Updated the VoteBuilder database and multiple Google spreadsheets daily to track volunteer recruitment and engagement
•	Sustained crucial field organizing efforts by maintaining a VoteBuilder database of over 100 volunteers, staffing over 80 voter outreach events and leading over 10 volunteer meetings.
•	Drafted email and social media content
•	Competencies developed: Project and people management, Self-sufficiency, Interpersonal skills, Event planning

education.description: I studied the International Baccalaureate for 2 years at Sevenoaks School and graduated with 44 points and a diploma.
education.activities:
Debate
Model United Nations

Psychology Society

featured.description:
I'm thrilled to share that I will be starting as an Operations Generalist at Anthropic! My start date is still tentative, but I'm aiming to start in August or September (just as soon as I recover from burnout). 

I am super excited to not only get exposure to one of the most pivotal industries of the century, artificial intelligence, but also do so in an environment uniquely well-suited for rapid operations upskilling: a start-up with an existing operations team (s/o to Jia Yuan Loke, Rebecca Raible, and Jamie Kerr). I'm also glad that Anthropic is a public benefit corporation, which is a reflection of how seriously everyone takes the social good here.

Finally, I'm grateful to the Anthropic team for welcoming me and providing an incredible end to my first (and overly-long!) job search.

You can read my full rationale here: https://lnkd.in/dhnMJAvQ

description.text: A shortened version of my resume.
description.accessibilityText: A shortened version of my resume.

featured.description:
Hi all,

As many of you know, I will be #graduating from UChicago at the end of May this year. I am still figuring out where I'd like to go next and am extending an open request for your help with any leads or contacts.

1️⃣ My dream job is EA community-building. Given that, I think I would be most interested in an entry-level operations/communications/HR position, ideally at an Effective Altruism-aligned start-up. (Yes, I will be a PA!)
2️⃣ I would like to be able to work from Chicago, although I can also work from Singapore or Hong Kong. I would prefer to stay in the U.S., but I will require work authorization next year.
3️⃣ I can start in June 2022

If you know of any job opportunities or leads that you might be able to share with me, please send them my way. 

Thanks in advance for any help or suggestions you all have!
